data.title: Charles Rhyee Maintains Hold Rating, Raises Price Target to $492 Amid Near-Term Strength and 2027 Metabolic Concentration Risks
data.description:
Charles Rhyee, an analyst from TD Cowen, maintained the Hold rating on Medpace Holdings. The associated price target was raised to $492.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Charles Rhyee has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to both solid near‑term execution and emerging medium‑term risks. On the positive side, he highlights improved bookings momentum, better commercial indicators such as higher RFP activity and win rates, and a healthier mix with oncology accounting for roughly half of recent awards, all of which support continued growth into 2026.

At the same time, Rhyee flags rising revenue concentration in a small number of sizable metabolic programs as a key overhang for 2027, since these trials will eventually roll off and management itself sees metabolic exposure stepping down from current levels. Although non‑metabolic revenue is growing and management expects both gross and net bookings to increase sequentially through 2026, the uncertain magnitude and timing of a potential metabolic-related revenue headwind in 2027 tempers his conviction. As a result, even with an upwardly revised price target to $492 based on his DCF work, he believes a balanced risk‑reward profile is best reflected in a Hold rating at this time.

Rhyee covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Cencora, Charles River Labs, and McKesson. According to TipRanks, Rhyee has an average return of 5.5% and a 51.42% success rate on recommended stocks.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Ovintiv Earns Buy Rating on Strong Free Cash Flow, Raised 2026 Production Guidance and Enhanced Capital Returns
data.description:
William Blair analyst Neal Dingmann has maintained their bullish stance on OVV stock, giving a Buy rating on July 17.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Neal Dingmann has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, notably Ovintiv’s stronger-than-expected free cash flow and EBITDAX, which were supported by higher liquids volumes, better realized liquids pricing, and disciplined capital spending. He also highlights management’s decision to lift 2026 production guidance while keeping capex unchanged, signaling growing confidence in the company’s long-term operational and financial trajectory.

Neal further points to Ovintiv’s stepped-up capital return strategy, including a sharp increase in share repurchases and a commitment to return more than 60% of free cash flow to shareholders, as a key component of his positive stance. In his view, the improved near-term production and capex outlook, combined with upcoming catalysts and a more robust shareholder return framework, positions the stock to outperform peers and supports a Buy recommendation.

According to TipRanks, Dingmann is an analyst with an average return of -2.9% and a 40.04% success rate. Dingmann covers the Energy sector, focusing on stocks such as Matador Resources, Ovintiv, and Northern Oil And Gas.

In another report released on July 17, Citi also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $66.00 price target.

data.title: Nasdaq Earns Buy Rating on Strong Q2 Beat, Broad-Based Growth, and Undervalued EPS Upside
data.description:
William Blair analyst Jeff Schmitt has maintained their bullish stance on NDAQ stock, giving a Buy rating on July 20.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Jeff Schmitt has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Nasdaq’s stronger-than-expected second-quarter performance and broad-based revenue growth. The company delivered a meaningful earnings beat, with especially robust expansion in its solutions businesses and its Capital Access Platforms unit, which is benefiting from product innovation, increased demand for data offerings, and early signs of a healthier listings pipeline.

Fintech operations, notably Verafin, are executing well as they roll out more advanced AI-driven tools, while market services are capitalizing on heightened trading activity and record options volumes. Management is also returning significant capital to shareholders, including through a new accelerated share repurchase, and is on track for substantial EPS growth in 2025 and 2026; yet, the stock still trades below its historical valuation range, reinforcing the upside case for a Buy rating.

Schmitt covers the Financial sector, focusing on stocks such as LPL Financial, Nasdaq, and Tradeweb Markets. According to TipRanks, Schmitt has an average return of 6.3% and a 52.31% success rate on recommended stocks.

data.title: Muted Growth and Rising Underwriting Pressure Prompt Hold Rating on RLI Shares
data.description:
William Blair analyst Adam Klauber has maintained their neutral stance on RLI stock, giving a Hold rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Adam Klauber has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors around RLI’s profitability and growth profile. The company’s recent earnings beat was driven largely by favorable reserve releases, while core underwriting metrics deteriorated as the underlying loss ratio and expense ratio both moved higher, signaling rising pressure on the combined ratio.

At the same time, premium growth is slowing, with property business facing persistent headwinds and casualty rate momentum decelerating, leading to an expectation of only low single‑digit top-line expansion and a notable EPS decline in 2026. Although RLI is still a strong performer versus peers and should sustain solid margins over the long run, the combination of muted growth, increasing underwriting pressure, and management’s cautious stance supports maintaining a neutral, or Hold, recommendation on the shares.

According to TipRanks, Klauber is a 3-star analyst with an average return of 2.2% and a 50.89% success rate. Klauber covers the Financial sector, focusing on stocks such as Progressive, Ryan Specialty Group, and HCI Group.

data.title: MediciNova: Differentiated NAFLD/MASH Profile and Complementary APOC3 Positioning Support Unchanged $6 Target and Buy Rating
data.description:
Medicinova, the Healthcare sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Jason McCarthy from Maxim Group maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a $6.00 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Jason McCarthy has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to MediciNova’s differentiated profile in metabolic disease. He notes that recent Phase 3 success by Arrowhead in severe hypertriglyceridemia validates APOC3 biology and the commercial potential of triglyceride-lowering therapies, while simultaneously underscoring that triglyceride reduction alone does not address liver fat or liver enzyme abnormalities.

In this context, MN-001’s oral, multi-mechanistic approach, which has shown the ability to lower both triglycerides and liver fat and to raise HDL in a diabetic NAFLD population, positions it for a potentially broader role in NAFLD/MASH with coexisting hypertriglyceridemia and type 2 diabetes. McCarthy also highlights the upcoming Phase 2 readout in this mixed population as a key catalyst and argues that MN-001 is complementary rather than directly competitive to APOC3 agents, supporting his unchanged $6 price target and Buy recommendation.

McCarthy covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Medicinova, Inmune Bio, and AIM ImmunoTech. According to TipRanks, McCarthy has an average return of -13.0% and a 30.06% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released on July 15, H.C. Wainwright also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $10.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Comfort Systems Earns Buy Rating on Major Earnings Beat, 73% Backlog Surge, and Strong Cash Generation
data.description:
William Blair analyst Tim Mulrooney has maintained their bullish stance on FIX stock, giving a Buy rating on July 9.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Tim Mulrooney has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including a notably strong earnings beat that exceeded already elevated investor expectations for both revenue and EPS. He believes the scale of this outperformance should prompt upward revisions to Street forecasts for 2026 and 2027, reinforcing a positive outlook for the company’s medium-term growth trajectory.

Mulrooney also highlights the 73% year-over-year surge in backlog, fueled by robust momentum in both mechanical and electrical services, as evidence of durable demand. The sequential $1.6 billion backlog increase, paired with a healthy 1.5x book-to-bill ratio and nearly $1 billion in free cash flow, supports his view that Comfort Systems has strong visibility, balance sheet flexibility, and earnings power, justifying a Buy recommendation.

Mulrooney covers the Industrials sector, focusing on stocks such as WillScot Mobile Mini Holdings, APi Group, and Brink’s Company. According to TipRanks, Mulrooney has an average return of 8.1% and a 52.86% success rate on recommended stocks.

data.title: Americas Gold and Silver: Buy Rating Reiterated, $9.75 Price Target Maintained on Operational Improvements and Galena Shaft Modernization
data.description:
Heiko Ihle, an analyst from H.C. Wainwright, reiterated the Buy rating on Americas Gold and Silver. The associated price target remains the same with $9.75.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Heiko Ihle has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that underscore Americas Gold and Silver’s improving operational profile and growth potential. The company’s second-quarter 2026 results showed solid silver-equivalent production despite a temporary disruption at Galena, and management reaffirmed full-year silver guidance with a clear bias toward stronger output in the second half of the year, supported by a robust cash position to fund ongoing reinvestment.

A key pillar of the thesis is the completion of Phase 2 of the No. 3 Shaft modernization at Galena, which materially enhances hoisting and throughput capacity and is expected to drive higher production, lower unit costs, and greater leverage to silver prices as the mine ramps up. Ihle’s valuation, based on a discounted cash flow approach with differentiated discount rates for each asset and a 2.0x NAV multiple in line with comparable peers, supports maintaining a $9.75 price target, reinforcing his conviction in the stock’s upside potential and justifying the reiterated Buy rating.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
